properties.Description: Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Xghajra, adjacent to the promenade Triq Dawret ix-Xatt. A rocky beach typical to the Maltese Coast surrounded by coastal garrigues, natural vegetation and concrete platforms with a slipway.

Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Xghajra at the end of promenade Triq Dawret ix-Xatt. A rocky beach typical to the Maltese coast surrounded by coastal garrigues, natural vegetation and concrete platforms.

Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Marsascala. This bathing site is part of the Marsascala Bay, located in Triq iz-Zonqor next to a water polo club. The site is surrounded by residential properties and a concrete platform.
properties.Name_MT: Triq iz-Zonqor opp Triq San Nikola
properties.Name_ENG: Zonqor Street opp San Nikola Street

properties.Description: Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Marsascala. This rocky bathing site is part of Marsascala Bay located in Zonqor street surrounded by residential premises.
Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Marsascala. This rocky bathing site is part of Marsascala Bay located in Triq is-Salini surrounded by residential premises.
Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Marsascala. This rocky bathing site is part of Marsascala Bay located in Triq is-Salini surrounded by salt pans.

Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Marsascala, adjacent to Triq il-Qalet. The site has a rocky and a sandy area. The source of the sand was obtained from dredging. There is a restaurant and a playground close by.
Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Marsascala, San Thomas Bay is a sandy beach surrounded by several boat houses and a restaurant.

Located along Malta’s southern coast in the fishing  village of Marsaxlokk , adjacent to Triq Tat-Trunciera.  This bay consists of a small sandy beach that is shouldered by tracts of pebbles, cobbles and rocky coast on either side of the beach.
Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Birzebbuga, adjacent to Triq il-Qajjenza. This rocky site forms part of St George's Bay and is surrounded by residential premises.

Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Birzebbuga, adjacent to Triq il-Qajjenza, next to Fort Ferretti. This rocky site forms part of St George's Bay and is surrounded by residential premises.
properties.Name_MT: Triq F.M. Ferretti  opp Triq S. Cachia Zammit
properties.Name_ENG: F.M. Ferretti street opp S. Cachia Zammit street

properties.Description: Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Birzebbuga, adjacent to Triq Ghar Dalam. This small sandy beach was replenished by dredging and forms part of St George's Bay. It is surrounded by residential premises and a restaurant.

Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Birzebbuga, adjacent to Triq Dawret il-Qalb Mqaddsa in the vicinity of the End of Cold War Memorial. This rocky site forms part of St George's Bay and is surrounded by residential premises.

Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Birzebbuga, adjacent to Triq il Bajja s-Sabiha and next to the waterpolo club. This sandy site with a concrete platform forms part of Pretty Bay, which was replenished and extended in the 1980s during dredging works at the site now occupied by the Malta Freeport Terminals.

Located along Malta’s southern coast in the village of Birzebbuga, adjacent to Triq il Bajja Sabiha. This sandy site forms part of the Pretty Bay, which was replenished and extended in the 1980s during dredging works at the site now occupied by the Malta Freeport Terminals. On the left hand side of the bay is composed of rock is composed of rocky terrain with some stretches of pebbles and concrete platforms/slipways. Surrounding the site are some restaurants and hotels.

Located along Malta’s southern coast in Wied iz-Zurrieq, Qrendi. The site comprises of a low lying rocky shoreline surrounded by steep sided gorge like valleys. Wied iz-Zurrieq is synonymous for diving, cuisine, fishing and tourism. Several divers visit the Um el Faroud wreck which is found in the vicinity.

Located along Malta’s southwestern coast at the limits of Qrendi and Siġġiewi.  It comprises two inlets, one of which is dominated by a large cave.  The terrestrial area at Ghar Lapsi has boat houses and two jetties.

Located along Malta’s northeastern coast within the locality of Bahar ic-Caghaq l/o Naxxar. This site is known as il-Qala ta’ Bahar ic-Caghaq with a small sandy beach representing the remnants of a former sand dune system. In the vicinity of the area are some restaurants.
properties.Name_ENG: Bahar Ic-Caghaq near the public toilets

properties.Description: Located along Malta’s northeastern coast in Pembroke. This bathing area is mainly a rocky coastline and close to a reverse osmosis plant.

Located along Malta’s northeastern coast in St. Julians. The Bay is a replenished sandy beach bound by gently sloping rocky shores. The beach was first replenished in 2004 and in 2005 using imported granite sand. The site is surrounded by commercial activity including hotels and restaurants.
properties.Name_MT: Il-Bajja ta’ San Gorg in-naha tax-xellug

properties.Description: Located along Malta’s northeastern coast in St George’s Bay. The Bay is a replenished sandy beach using imported granite sand.
properties.Name_MT: Il-Bajja ta’ San Gorg in-naha tal-lemin

properties.Description: Located along Malta’s northeastern coast next to Dragonara Point adjacent to Wilga Street, St Julians . The rocky area is protected as an Area of Ecological Importance as it harbours an important transitional coastal wetland.
Located along Malta’s northeast coast in  St. Julians Bay, St. Julians adjacent to Triq Spinola.  Surrounded by urban development, residential properties, restaurant, hotels and concreate platforms.
Located along Malta’s northeastern coast along the promenade of Gorg Borg Olivier street. This rocky site is surrounded by private residents, restaurants and next to a waterpolo club.
properties.Name_MT: Biswit il-Kazin tal-Waterpolo ta’ San Ġiljan
properties.Name_ENG: next to St. Julian's Waterpolo Club

properties.Description: Located along Malta’s northeast coast  adjacent to Triq Censu Tabone. The area has a small sandy beach and is surrounded with restaurants, hotels, private residences and a waterpolo club.
Located along Malta’s northeast coast adjacent to Triq Censu Tabone. The area has a small sandy beach and is surrounded with restaurants, hotels and private residences.

Located along Malta’s northeastern coast adjacent to Tower road, Sliema. It includes a small sandy/pebble beach, a jetty, a slipway and also natural rocky coast. The area is surrounded with playground, public gardens, restaurants, hotels and private residences.
Located along Malta’s northeast coast this site is a typical rocky beach located along the promenade of Tower road. The area is surrounded with playground, public gardens and a waterpolo club.
properties.Name_ENG: Exiles beach next to children's playground

properties.Description: Located along Malta’s northeastern coast adjacent to the promenade of Tower Road, Sliema. The bay is a typical rocky beach and has clear, turquoise waters with bathing pools carved into the rocks. This rocky site is surrounded by hotels, restaurants, lidos, and residential areas.

Located next to Ghar id-Dud promenade located beneath the Tower Road, Sliema on the Malta’s northeastern coast. The rocky site is surrounded by hotels, restaurants, lidos, residential areas and a waterpolo club.
Located along Malta’s northeast coast large areas along the promenade of Qui Si Sana Road corner with Tower road, Sliema. The area is surrounded by a number or restaurants and high rise buildings.
Located along Malta’s northeast coast large areas along the promenade of Qui Si Sana Road. The area is surrounded by a playground, restaurants, lidos and high rise buildings.

Located along Malta’s western coast, the inlet of Ġnejna Bay, Imgarr forms part of the landward valley. On the side of this sandy beach with cobbled stones there is a cemented area. The area is surrounded by agricultural fields and next to the shore there are boat houses and kiosk.

Located along Malta’s western coast at Imgarr. This sandy beach incorporates a small cobbled area and the terrain at either side of these beach is characterized by rocky shores. Accesibale from a steep stairs from Triq Ghajn Tuffieha, Mellieha.
properties.Name_MT: Il-Bajja ta' Ghajn Tuffieha in-naha tax-xellug

properties.Description: Located along Malta’s western coast at Imgarr.  A sandy beach with the rock shore terrain on the side of these beach houses a resturant. The bay is accesibale from a steep stairs from Triq Ghajn Tuffieha, Mellieha.
properties.Name_MT: Il-Bajja ta' Ghajn Tuffieha in-naha tal-lemin

properties.Description: Located along Malta’s western coast adjacent to Triq in-Nahhalija limits of Mellieha. This sandy beach is classified with rock shores at either side.  Golden Sands is the second largest beach in Malta and has rare and valuable sand dunes which not only replenish the beach but also act as an important habitat for endemic species.

Located along Malta’s western coast adjacent to Triq in-Nahhalija limits of Mellieha. This sandy beach is classified with rock shores at either side.  Golden Sands is the second largest beach in Malta and has rare and valuable sand dunes which not only replenish the beach but also act as an important habitat for endemic species. Close to the area is a hotel, kiosk and restaurants.

Located along Malta’s northwestern coast next to Triq il-Prajjiet at the limits of Mellieha. The Bay is surrounded by rocky slopes on either side, with a sandy beach and a break water, whilst the seabed within the bay consists of a blend of sand and rocky patches with minor pebble tracts at the mouth of the Bay.  The recreational hamlet at Anchor Bay is privately owned and has been converted to a mini theme park and an open-air museum.

Located along Malta’s northern coast of Cirkewwa and is accessible from a steep stairs adjacent to the parking area in Triq il Latinja. The picturesque site includes consists of a small sandy beach within a natural cove and rugged cliff-side scenery. A restaurant is located on the side of the bay.

Located along Malta’s northern coast of Cirkewwa in the vicinity of the Gozo ferry Terminal and adjacent to Triq il-Marfa. This sandy beach overlooks the islands of Comino and Gozo and is also predominantly composed of sand and rocky coastline. Close to the site is a hotel and some restaurants.

Located along Malta’s northern coast adjacent to Triq Ramlet il-Qortin. This bay is a typical sandy beach which is divided by a rocky coast. The surrounding landscape presents a rustic setting with several rubble walls and corbelled huts. The area is surrounded by lido, kiosk and restaurants.

Located in the northern cost most part of Malta in Triq l-Armier Mellieha. A sandy beach which is divided by a rocky coast. The surrounding landscape presents a rustic setting with several rubble walls and corbelled huts. The area is surrounded by lido and kiosk.

Located along Malta’s northern coast in Triq l-Marfa Mellieha. This is the largest sandy beach in Malta situated adjacent to the Ghadira Natural Reserve which was set up in 1978 to protect migratory birds.  The reserve provides wetland and salt marsh habitats. The bay is surrounded by a u-shaped valley which is rich in agricultural activities. The area is surrounded by hotels and restaurants.

Located along Malta’s northern coast in Triq l-Marfa Mellieha. This is the largest sandy beach in Malta situated adjacent to the Ghadira Natural Reserve which was set up in 1978 to protect migratory birds.  The reserve provides wetland and salt marsh habitats. The bay is surrounded by a u-shaped valley which is rich in agricultural activities. The area is surrounded by hotels and restaurants.
Located along Malta’s northern coast next to the largest sandy beach in Malta in Triq l- Marfa Mellieha. This bathing site is a cement jetty and is surrounded by restaurants.
Located along Malta’s northern coast in Triq Dawrett It-Tunnara Mellieha adjacent to the Tunnara museum (Westreme Battery) . The area is surrounded by private residences and restuarants.
Located along Malta’s north eastern coast acceasible from the side road of Triq il-Mistra at St Pauls Bay.  A rocky shore surrounded by natural vegetation.

Located along Malta’s northeastern coast accessible from the side road of Triq il-Mistra at St Pauls Bay. This beach is situated at the end of the valley and is covered with pebbles and sand. In the vicinity there is ‘Ix-Xaghra tal-Kortin’, which comprises a mosaic of boulders, stones, dry gorges and crevices.
Located along Malta’s north-eastern coast, in Triq il-Fekruna St Pauls Bay, comprised of a cement jetti. The area is also surrounded by private dwellings and boat houses.

Located along Malta’s north-eastern coast of Xemxija Hill St Pauls Bay.   A rocky coastline shore with  a number of small boats moored in the area. This area is surrounded by private dwellings and restaurant.
properties.Name_MT: Biswit il-lido fi Triq Telghet ix-Xemxija
properties.Name_ENG: Next to lido at Xemxija Hill

properties.Description: Located along Malta’s northeastern coast of Xemxija outside the area of Veccja Wharf at St Pauls Bay. Tal-Veccja area is situated on the left side is, bordered by a breakwater which in turn shelters a mooring area for small boats.  The bathing area at this location is surrounded by some very steep rock faces with some crevices present at intervals.
Located along Malta’s north-eastern coast, adjacent to Triq Settla Maris St Pauls Bay.  There are private residences and a waterpolo club nearby.

Located along Malta’s northeastern coast at the end of Sqaq tax-Xema St Pauls Bay. Consists of a small sandy beach which is surrounded by boulder scree and maquis areas on one side and a concrete ramp on the opposite side.  The bathing water can be accessed either via a ramp or via a flight of stairs at the side of a building that is used as a yacht club. The area is surrounded with private residences and a school.

Located along Malta’s northeastern coast in Triq San Frangisk St Pauls Bay. The stretch of rocky cove that lies between the Wignacourt Tower and the rocky point below the Bocci club, south of the Tower, is known as il-Qala tal-Ghazzelin, St Pauls Bay.  The seabed in this area is predominantly characterised by rock and coral outcrops. The area is surrounded with private dwellings and some restaurant.

Located in the area known as Tas- Sirens on the north-eastern coast in Triq San Geraldu, St Pauls Bay .  A series of rock-cut bathing pools are scattered along this stretch of coast. Private residences and a waterpolo club in the area.

Located along Malta’s north eastern coast at the beginning of Bugibba promenade. A  rocky beach with Roman Salt Pans and a breakwater. The site is situated in an urban area surrounded by a number of private residences, restaurants and hotels.

Located along Malta’s northeastern coast adjacent to Triq Dawret il-Gzejjer, St Pauls Bay. This is a rocky beach with a small sandy patch and cemented area, including a slipway. This beach is surrounded by several commercial activities such as restaurants, hotels and private residences.

Located along Malta’s northeastern coast adjacent to Triq Dawret il-Gzejjar St Pauls Bay. This sand beach was a linear rocky shore with a sharp rock surface, however, in 2006 it was replenished with artificial sand. Artificial sand has been placed over approximately 2,000 square meters of rocky coast thus transforming the rocky coast into a sandy beach.  The area is surrounded by hotels, restaurants and a lido.
properties.Name_MT: Il-Bajja Ta Taht il-Latnija ta' Bugibba

properties.Description: Located at Malta’s northeastern coast adjacent to the Bugibba/Qawra Promenade. A typical rocky shore with clear turquoise waters and smooth flat rocks, ideal for snorkelling. Access to the bay is through a concrete platform or going down a steep hill. The area is surrounded by hotels, restaurants and lidos.
Located along Malta’s north eastern coast adjacent to the Bugibba/Qawra Promenade. A rocky shore with a cement jetty, surrounded by restaurants, hotels and private dwellings.
Located along Malta’s north eastern coast adjacent to the Bugibba/Qawra Promenade. A rocky shore surrounded by restaurants, hotels and private dwellings.

Located along Malta’s north eastern coast adjacent to the Bugibba/Qawra Promenade. A rocky shore with a cement jetty and a slipway, surrounded by restaurants, hotels, boat houses and private dwellings.
Located along Malta’s north eastern coast with a small sandy beach surrounded by rocky shore. The bay is adjacent to Coast Road and opposite a hotel.

Located along Gozo’s southern coast in Ghajnsielem. The name means red quay as translated from "Xatt l-Ahmar" This is an ideal spot for both swimming and diving, mostly frequented by locals, known for the crystal water and beautiful coastal surroundings.
Located along Gozo’s southern coast in Sannat adjacent to Triq ta' Mgarr Ix-Xini. A rocky beach with pebbles that is characterized by deep valleys located on either side of the bay.
Located along Gozo’s southern coast in Sannat. This is a private beach surrounded by natural landscaping and a small restuarant.

Located along Gozo’s southern coast adjacent to Triq il-Kantra in Munxar.  The rocky shore is connected to a concrete promenade which leads to il-Kantra Valley.  Xlendi bay is an inlet flanked by steep cliffs. The bay is also surrounded by natural vegetation, rock escarpment and several agricultural fields.

Located along Gozo’s southern coast adjacent to Triq San Xmun in Munxar.  This area consists of a rocky coast with a concrete promenade in front of Ghar ta' Karolina.  Xlendi bay is an inlet flanked by steep cliffs. The bay is also surrounded by natural vegetation, rock escarpment and several agricultural fields.

Located along Gozo’s southern coast in Munxar adjacent to Triq ir-Rabat.  The Bay consists of a small pebble beach and it is an inlet flanked by steep cliffs.  Surrounded by natural vegetation, resturants and hotels.
Located along Gozo’s southern coast of Munxar Triq ir-Rabat.  The Bay consists of a small pebble beach and an inlet flanked by steep cliffs, surrounded by natural vegetation, hotels and restaurants.

Located along Gozo’s western coast of San Lawrenz which is accessed either by boat via a cave tunnel or through a road leading from the main parking area. It comprises of an enclosed pebble beach within Qawra’s quasi-circular solution subsidence structure and the inland sea is relatively sheltered and not directly exposed to the coast. The terrestrial area at Dwejra has conserved most of its natural context, with geological, geomorphological, ecological, archaeological, historical and aesthetic

Located along Gozo’s northern coast in Triq ix-Xwejni, Zebbug, and can be accessed either via the road that snakes down from Zebbuġ or along the coastal road through Marsalforn. In this bay there is a protruding headland on which a structure known as il-Qolla il-Bajda can be found. This is a pebbled beach with scores of salt pans cut into the rock throughout the shore platform are present in the area surrounding the bay. There are also restaurants in the area.

Located along Gozo’s northern coast in Triq ix-Xwejni corner with Triq Santa Marija, Zebbug, and can be accessed either via the road that snakes down from Zebbug or along the coastal road through Marsalforn. In this bay there is a protruding headland on which a structure known as il-Qolla il-Bajda can be found. This is shallow bay with small pebbly beach and rocky stretches. It is an excellent bathing area enjoyed by locals and visitors and it is also a popular place for diving. it There are res

Located along Gozo’s northern coast in Triq Santa Marija, Zebbug forming part of il-Bajja ta’ Marsalforn. The bay’s geology is susceptible to accelerated coastal erosion since most of Marsalforn’s coast is made up of globigerina formation.  The various bathing water areas around this site are surrounded by private residences.
properties.Name_ENG: From Breakwater to St Marija Street

properties.Description: Located along Gozo’s northern coast in Triq Santa Marija, Zebbug forming part of il-Bajja ta’ Marsalforn. The bay’s geology is susceptible to accelerated coastal erosion since most of Marsalforn’s coast is made up of globigerina formation.  The various bathing water areas around this site are surrounded by private residences.
properties.Name_MT: Triq Santa Marija opp Triq Tax-Xtut
properties.Name_ENG: St Marija Street opp Tax Xtut Street

properties.Description: Located along Gozo’s northern coast in Triq il-Port, Zebbug. A sandy beach with small pebbles backed by a promenade which runs right around the head of the bay. The rocks along each side of the bay provide plenty of additional space for sunbathing and swimming straight into deeper water. A number of marine, touristic and recreational amenities are available.
Located along Gozo’s northern coast in Triq Ghar Qawqla, Zebbug. This bathing site is surrounding by rocky coast and pebbles. The area is very popular with divers and for training purposes.

Located along Gozo’s northern coast at the end of Triq il Ghajn Qasab, Xaghra. Ir-Ramla l-Hamra is characterised by a reddish sandy beach and sand dunes, this being the largest remaining sand dune system in the Maltese Islands. Ir-Ramla il-Hamra is situated at the mouth of a wide river valley. The area is dominated by the sandy beach and associated sand dunes whilst the surrounding landscape is made up of gently sloping hills, blue clay slopes on the western side and exposed cliffs on either si

Located along Gozo’s northern coast at the end of Triq il Ghajn Qasab, Xaghra. Ir-Ramla il-Hamra is characterised by a reddish sandy beach and sand dune, this being the largest remaining sand dune system in the Maltese Islands. Ir-Ramla l-Hamra is situated at the mouth of a wide river valley. The area is dominated by the sandy beach and associated sand dunes whilst the surrounding landscape is made up of gently sloping hills, blue clay slopes on the western side and exposed cliffs on either sid

Located along Gozo’s northern coast, at the end of Triq San Blas, Nadur. The sandy beach at il-Bajja ta’ San Blas is situated in a secluded and quiet area, surrounded by rocky landscape.  This makes the bay unique and spectacular. Access to the beach is only on foot, via a very steep, narrow pathway.  The sand is reddish-orange, similar to that of ir-Ramla il-Hamra, attained from the presence of the Green Sand layer outcrops.

Located along Gozo’s northern coast in Nadur between Qala and Nadur. Dahlet Qorrot is a small inlet nestled in the northern cliff face of Gozo and can be reached from Nadur. Scenery at this site includes extensive views of rock fall formations (Rdum). Several boat houses present in the vicinity.

Located along Gozo’s southern coast in Triq il-Kuncizzjoni, Qala. A typical rocky beach with a small sandy and pebbled area with crystal -clear water. The area isn’t highly urbanised and is located in an unspoilt and natural setting. This bay provides several water sports activities such as wind surfing, diving and yachting.
It is a small rocky bay. Located along Gozo’s southern coast, adjacent to the Mgarr yacht marina and outside the left arm of the breakwater at Mgarr harbour, Ghajnsielem.

Located in between the islands of Comino and Cominotto, which forms part of Natura 2000. The shallow, transparent, cyan waters which characterise the Blue Lagoon area amongst the most beautiful and picturesque scenery in the Maltese Islands.  For this reason, the area attracts a large number of tourists and Maltese, especially during the summer season. Vessel operators that link the sister islands to each other daily, from fishing, yachting, recreational and diving are available. The terrestrial

Located on the Island of Commino which form part of Natura 2000. A sandy beach and the seabed's within the bays are mainly composed of white sand with some rock and coral outcrops. The area is surrounded by natural vegetation and a touristic complex.

Located in Comino which forms part of Natura 2000. A small sandy beach with crystal clear water surrounded by sand dunes and surrounded by vegetation and tamarisk trees. A public camping site and sanitary facilities are located some meters away from the beach.
Located along Malta’s northern coast in Triq Dahlet ix- Xmajjar, Armier. A typical rocky beach with a campsite in the vicinity.
Located along Malta’s northeastern coast in Bahar ic-Caghaq. A rocky beach situated behind the largest water park of the Maltese Islands.
Located along Gozo’s western coast opposite the Fungus Rock in Dwejra Bay San Lawrenz.
Located along Malta’s northeast coast on Tigne Seafront, Sliema, below one of the main shopping complex in the Maltese Island.
properties.Name_MT: Tigne Point Slipway taht il-pont tal-pedonali
properties.Name_ENG: Tigne Point Slipway under the pedestrian bridge

properties.Description: Located along Gozo’s southern coast in Ghajsielem. The beach is located in a natural place, surrounded by cliffs.
Located along Malta’s northern coast in Selmun. A sandy beach at the bottom of the hill in a rural environment being Selmun Tower.
Located along Malta’s northern coast, a typical rock shore adjacent to the Promenade of Marfa Road, Cirkewwa.
Located along Gozo’s southern coast in Ghajnsielem . A rocky shore next to a diving spot.
properties.Name_MT: Ras il-Hobz Dahla ta’ Mgarr ix Xini
properties.Name_ENG: Ras il-Hobz Dahla ta' Mgarr ix Xini

properties.Description: Located along Maltese’s southern coast in Triq il- Wilga, Marsaxlokk. This is a sandy beach.
Located along Malta’s southern coast in Xaghjra, next to the sandy beach and Malta's film set.
Located along Malta’s southern coast Marsascala at the end of the road next to Sant Tumas Bay. A pebbled and rocky beach.
Located along Malta’s northern coast  in the vicinity of Ramlet il- Qortin, Armier. A typical rocky shore.
properties.Name_ENG: Armier- next to Ramla Tal-Qortin Bay

properties.Description: Located along Malta’s southern coast, adjacent to parking area at the end of the road of Triq San Patrizju. A flat rocky shore .
Located along Malta’s northern coast adjacent to White Tower Bay. A typical rocky shore.
Located along Gozo's northern coast adjacent to the public toilets. A typical rocky shore.
Located along Malta’s southern coast in corner with Triq Ghar ix-Xama Marsascla. A typical rock shore.

properties.Name_MT: Xatt it-Tiben u Qrib Triq il-Kbira Siega
properties.Name_ENG: Hay Wharf and Near Great Siega Road

properties.Description: Located along Malta’s northern coast in Triq l-Marfa Mellieha. This is the largest sandy beach in Malta situated adjacent to the Ghadira Natural Reserve which was set up in 1978 to protect migratory birds.  The reserve provides wetland and salt marsh habitats. The bay is surrounded by a u-shaped valley which is rich in agricultural activities. The area is surrounded by hotels and restaurants.
